channel.title: npj Quantum Materials
channel.description:
Open for Submissions

Publishing high-quality research on quantum materials and condensed matter.

npj Quantum Materials is a fully open-access journal that aims to publish work that significantly advances understanding of the fundamental properties, fabrication and applications of quantum materials.

npj Quantum Materials covers topics including, but not limited to, superconductivity and superconducting materials, correlated electronic physics and materials, topological quantum physics and materials, quantum phenomena in advanced energy materials, condensed matter, and the materials science of advanced quantum technologies.

In addition to original research, the journal welcomes critical Reviews, and thought-provoking Perspectives and Comment articles.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Phonon bunching induced strong four-phonon scattering for low lattice thermal conductivity in layered Na<sub>2</sub>CoSe<sub>2</sub>O
item.content:encoded:
npj Quantum Materials, Published online: 08 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41535-025-00832-w

Phonon bunching induced strong four-phonon scattering for low lattice thermal conductivity in layered Na2CoSe2O
item.dc:title: Phonon bunching induced strong four-phonon scattering for low lattice thermal conductivity in layered Na<sub>2</sub>CoSe<sub>2</sub>O

item.title: Interplay between altermagnetism and superconductivity in two dimensions: intertwined symmetries and singlet-triplet mixing
item.content:encoded:
npj Quantum Materials, Published online: 30 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41535-025-00840-w

Interplay between altermagnetism and superconductivity in two dimensions: intertwined symmetries and singlet-triplet mixing
item.dc:title: Interplay between altermagnetism and superconductivity in two dimensions: intertwined symmetries and singlet-triplet mixing
